reflection_enumeration.description: gRPC reflection enumeration -- discover services, methods, and message types via server reflection API

probes.description: gRPC reflection list services -- query ServerReflection/ServerReflectionInfo to enumerate all registered gRPC services and methods
gRPC-Web endpoint detection -- check if endpoint accepts gRPC-Web content type, indicating protobuf service

message_manipulation.description: gRPC message type manipulation -- send malformed or unexpected protobuf messages to trigger errors or bypass validation

probes.description: Unknown field injection -- add extra protobuf fields not in the expected schema; server may process them if using permissive deserialization
Type confusion in protobuf -- send field with wrong wire type (e.g., string where int expected) to trigger deserialization errors revealing internal structure

auth_bypass.description: gRPC authentication bypass -- test for missing or weak auth on gRPC endpoints

probes.description: gRPC call without auth metadata -- send request without Authorization/token metadata to test for unauthenticated access
gRPC admin method access -- attempt to call admin/internal gRPC methods discovered via reflection without elevated credentials

field_type_injection.description: Protobuf field-type injection — int32 → repeated int32 mismatch causes array append, string → bytes causes binary handling, optional → required omission triggers default-value paths.

probes.description: Send `id` as repeated int32 instead of singular — protobuf may concatenate, last-wins, or first-wins differently across implementations. Useful for IDOR via array smuggling.
Send `role` field as bytes (wire type 2) instead of enum (wire type 0). Some servers fail-open and accept any role, leaking admin path.
Field number collision — send TWO fields with same field number but different wire types. Some implementations process the first, others the last.

stream_cancellation_race.description: gRPC stream cancellation race — client sends RST_STREAM mid-call. Some servers commit the operation before honoring cancellation, allowing partial-state attacks.

probes.description: [Manual] Open server-streaming RPC, send RST_STREAM after first message. If server logs full operation despite cancellation, race-condition primitive established.

transcoding_open_redirect.description: gRPC-Web transcoding open redirect — Envoy/grpc-gateway maps HTTP path to gRPC service. Service path injection can redirect to attacker-controlled service if transcoding rules are loose.

probes.description: Direct gRPC service path — bypasses HTTP-route-level auth that doesn't apply at gRPC layer.
Path traversal in transcoded path — Envoy/gateway may resolve traversal before applying auth filter chain.

deadline_strip.description: Strip grpc-timeout / x-envoy-upstream-rq-timeout-ms headers — some servers default to no timeout, allowing slowloris-style DoS or long-running query abuse.

probes.description: Remove grpc-timeout. Send query that should hit the per-RPC deadline. If response time exceeds normal max, deadline is server-default (often unlimited).
